message: bd-1nmm: automate Winget publication + Windows packaging docs

 {"id":"bd-1b8k","title":"E2E: policy guardrails (rate limit, blast radius, max kills)","description":"## Goal
Validate safety guardrails end-to-end with detailed JSONL logging.

## Scope
- Rate limiting: per-minute/hour/session counters, cooldown warnings, and hard blocks.
- Blast radius warnings: ensure high-impact actions require explicit confirmation paths.
- Max kills per session: verify guardrail enforcement in plan/apply.
- Negative paths: malformed policy.json, missing policy file, override attempts logged.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages are clear and actionable.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, guardrail_type, threshold, observed, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: plan outputs, apply outputs (dry-run), stderr snapshots, manifest.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Guardrails are enforced in plan/apply flows.
- [ ] Warnings and blocks are surfaced clearly to users.
- [ ] Override attempts are logged with explicit user intent markers.
- [ ] Error paths produce correct exit codes and guidance.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate.

## Test Plan
- E2E script using shared harness + fixtures: run `pt agent plan/apply --dry-run` against crafted inputs.

 {"id":"bd-1o51","title":"E2E: output formatting modes (fields/compact/TOON/truncation)","description":"## Goal
Validate CLI output-mode behavior end-to-end with detailed JSONL logs + manifests.

## Scope
- `pt agent plan --fields minimal|standard|full` validation.
- `--compact` + `--format toon/json` output validation.
- `--max-tokens` truncation + continuation token resume.
- Negative paths: invalid fields, invalid continuation tokens, invalid format.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages are clear and actionable.
- Consume goldens/fixtures produced by bd-1v91 + bd-z3ma.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, exit_code, token_count, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: raw outputs, schema validation reports, stderr snapshots, manifest.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Output modes validated against schema and deterministic ordering.
- [ ] Truncation + continuation behaviors verified.
- [ ] Invalid inputs fail with clear errors + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate.

## Test Plan
- E2E script via shared harness and fixtures.

 {"id":"bd-1rf2","title":"E2E: session diff + compare workflows","description":"## Goal
Exercise session diff/compare paths end-to-end with JSONL logs and negative-path validation.

## Scope
- `pt diff --last` on two sessions from fixtures.
- `pt agent diff` with JSON output verification.
- Failure paths: missing session, invalid session id, schema mismatch.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages.
- Validate diff summary fields and delta counts.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs with command, session_ids, delta_summary, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: diff outputs (json + human), validation report, stderr snapshots.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Diff outputs schema-validate and are deterministic.
- [ ] Failure paths produce clear errors + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted for each diff path.

## Test Plan
- E2E script creates two fixture sessions and runs diff commands.

 {"id":"bd-1sj4","title":"No-mock policy enforcement (denylist/allowlist + CI gate)","description":"## Goal
Enforce a no-mock policy for core modules with automated checks.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Define denylist of mock crates/usages for core modules (collect, inference, decision, plan, action).
- Allowlist in test-only helpers and explicitly mock-friendly modules.
- CI job to fail if denylisted mocks appear in restricted paths.
- Provide lint/test helper to scan for mock usage patterns.
- Document the no-mock policy and exception process.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Denylist/allowlist defined and documented.
- [ ] CI gate fails on forbidden mock usage in core modules.
- [ ] Local check command documented.

## Test Plan
- Unit: policy parser/validator for denylist rules.
- Integration: intentionally introduce mock usage in a fixture and verify CI fails.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:00:08.269672186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:09:16.818654162Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:09:16.818361398Z","close_reason":"Implemented no-mock policy check, docs, CI gate","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sj4","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:00:08.282524208Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -222,6 +236,79 @@ jobs:
             ```
           labels: automated,release
 
+  # ==========================================================================
+  # Update Winget package repo (create PR)
+  # ==========================================================================
+  update-winget:
+    name: Update Winget Package Repo
+    needs: [generate-packages, test-packages]
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 10
+    if: github.event.inputs.dry_run != 'true' && needs.generate-packages.outputs.winget_generated == 'true' && secrets.WINGET_PKGS_TOKEN != ''
+
+    steps:
+      - name: Download package files
+        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: package-files
+          path: packages
+
+      - name: Checkout Winget repo
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          repository: ${{ env.WINGET_PKGS_REPO }}
+          token: ${{ secrets.WINGET_PKGS_TOKEN }}
+          path: winget
+
+      - name: Update Winget manifests
+        run: |
+          version="${{ needs.generate-packages.outputs.version }}"
+          manifest_dir="winget/manifests/p/ProcessTriage/pt/${version}"
+
+          mkdir -p "$manifest_dir"
+
+          cp packages/pt.winget.yaml "${manifest_dir}/ProcessTriage.pt.yaml"
+          cp packages/pt.winget.installer.yaml "${manifest_dir}/ProcessTriage.pt.installer.yaml"
+          cp packages/pt.winget.locale.en-US.yaml "${manifest_dir}/ProcessTriage.pt.locale.en-US.yaml"
+
+          # Defensive check for missed substitutions.
+          if grep -R --line-number '{{' "$manifest_dir"; then
+            echo "::error::Unreplaced template placeholder detected in Winget manifests"
+            exit 1
+          fi
+
+          cd winget
+          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
+          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
+
+          if git diff --quiet; then
+            echo "No changes to Winget manifests"
+            exit 0
+          fi
+
+          branch="update-process-triage-winget-${version}"
+          git checkout -b "$branch"
+          git add "manifests/p/ProcessTriage/pt/${version}"
+          git commit -m "Add ProcessTriage.pt ${version}"
+
+          echo "branch=$branch" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
+
+      - name: Create PR to Winget repo
+        if: env.branch != ''
+        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v7
+        with:
+          token: ${{ secrets.WINGET_PKGS_TOKEN }}
+          path: winget
+          branch: ${{ env.branch }}
+          title: "Add ProcessTriage.pt ${{ needs.generate-packages.outputs.version }}"
+          body: |
+            Automated update of ProcessTriage.pt Winget manifests to version ${{ needs.generate-packages.outputs.version }}.
+
+            Release: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/v${{ needs.generate-packages.outputs.version }}
+
+            If this repo is a fork/mirror, open a follow-up PR to `microsoft/winget-pkgs`.
+          labels: automated,release,winget
+
   # ==========================================================================
   # Test package installation (dry run validation)
   # ==========================================================================

             echo "  Linux x86_64: OK"

-            echo "  Linux x86_64: MISSING (expected for dry runs)"
+            echo "  Linux x86_64: MISSING (allowed for dry runs)"
+          fi
+
+          if [[ ! -f packages/pt.winget.installer.yaml ]]; then
+            echo "Winget URL validation skipped (no Winget manifests generated)."
+            exit 0
+          fi
+
+          # x64 is required whenever Winget manifests are generated.
+          x64_url=$(sed -n '/Architecture: x64/{n;s/^[[:space:]]*InstallerUrl:[[:space:]]*//p;q;}' packages/pt.winget.installer.yaml)
+          if [[ -z "$x64_url" ]]; then
+            echo "::error::Missing x64 InstallerUrl in pt.winget.installer.yaml"
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          if curl --head --fail --silent "$x64_url" > /dev/null; then
+            echo "  Winget Windows x64 URL: OK"
+          else
+            echo "::error::Winget Windows x64 URL is not reachable: $x64_url"
+            exit 1
+          fi
+
+          # arm64 is optional. If present, it must resolve.
+          arm64_url=$(sed -n '/Architecture: arm64/{n;s/^[[:space:]]*InstallerUrl:[[:space:]]*//p;q;}' packages/pt.winget.installer.yaml || true)
+          if [[ -n "$arm64_url" ]]; then
+            if curl --head --fail --silent "$arm64_url" > /dev/null; then
+              echo "  Winget Windows arm64 URL: OK"
+            else
+              echo "::error::Winget Windows arm64 URL is not reachable: $arm64_url"
+              exit 1
+            fi
+          else
+            echo "  Winget Windows arm64 URL: not present (optional)"
           fi
 ```

 - Linux aarch64 (ARM64)
 - macOS x86_64 (Intel)
 - macOS aarch64 (Apple Silicon)
+- Windows x86_64 (Winget/Scoop packaging path)

+For Windows package publication and maintainer token requirements, see

 ---
 
 ## What Problem Does This Solve?

@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+# Windows Packaging Operations
+
+This document covers the release-time workflow for Windows package publication.
+
+## Channels
+
+- `Scoop`: `process-triage/scoop-bucket`
+- `Winget`: dedicated repo/fork path, with optional upstream PR to `microsoft/winget-pkgs`
+- `Chocolatey`: no automated publication path yet (manual/issue-tracked until parity work is scheduled)
+
+## Automation Flow
+
+`update-packages.yml` performs:
+
+1. Generate package artifacts from release checksums (`pt.json`, optional Winget manifests).
+2. Validate manifests and URLs.
+3. Open PRs to Homebrew and Scoop repos.
+4. If Winget manifests were generated, open a PR to the configured Winget repo.
+
+## Winget Requirements
+
+- Windows `x64` asset is required whenever Winget manifests are generated.
+- Windows `arm64` is optional. If present in the manifest, URL validation must pass.
+- Validation is run in CI before the Winget update job.
+
+## Required Secrets
+
+- `WINGET_PKGS_TOKEN`: token with push/PR permissions for `${WINGET_PKGS_REPO}`.
+- `SCOOP_BUCKET_TOKEN`: existing Scoop publication token.
+- `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN`: existing Homebrew publication token.
+
+If `WINGET_PKGS_TOKEN` is absent, Winget publication is skipped.
+
+## Winget Repo Target
+
+Workflow env default:
+
+- `WINGET_PKGS_REPO=process-triage/winget-pkgs`
+
+This can be changed to another fork/repo. For direct upstream contribution,
+maintainers can set it to `microsoft/winget-pkgs` if token permissions allow.
+
+## Expected Winget Path Layout
+
+For version `X.Y.Z`, manifests are written to:
+
+- `manifests/p/ProcessTriage/pt/X.Y.Z/ProcessTriage.pt.yaml`
+- `manifests/p/ProcessTriage/pt/X.Y.Z/ProcessTriage.pt.installer.yaml`
+- `manifests/p/ProcessTriage/pt/X.Y.Z/ProcessTriage.pt.locale.en-US.yaml`
+
+## Maintainer Checklist
+
+1. Ensure release includes Windows x64 asset (arm64 optional).
+2. Confirm `checksums.sha256` contains Windows entries.
+3. Ensure `WINGET_PKGS_TOKEN` is configured.
+4. Verify PR created by `Update Package Repos` workflow.
+5. If using a fork/dedicated repo, open or update upstream Winget PR as needed.
